what_it_does: Defines a mobile-style HTML screen titled All Pieces, showing a workshop ledger with search text, category pills, two piece sections, generated rows with version labels, a count strip, a compose action, and an icon rail.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
Uses shared stylesheets at /surface/styles/piece.css and /surface/styles/phone.css plus a small file-local style block.
Renders inside a .screen phone shell with status bar, page header, search affordance, pills, sections, rows, count strip, action, and rail.
Uses DNA.org.icon for the search icon and bottom rail icons after loading /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
Populates row lists from hardcoded JavaScript arrays A and B.
Uses warm color CSS variables including gold, ochre, bronze, and bronze-line.
standouts: The ledger data is embedded directly in the file and rendered through a small row template function.
The bottom rail highlights the ledger icon with var(--gold).
The visible metadata combines piece title, substrate-style descriptors, warmth values, take/screen counts, and version tags.
conventions: Follows a compact prototype pattern with static HTML structure and inline JavaScript hydration.
Uses CSS custom properties from the surrounding surface design system.
Uses abbreviated class names such as p-hd, p-sup, p-h, p-row, and p-rail.
Breaks semantic form convention by presenting search as a div rather than an input.
concerns: Displayed counts describe 12, 26, and 38 pieces, while this file only populates six visible rows.
The search affordance has no input behavior in this file.
The compose action is a div with no click behavior in this file.
Icon-only rail items have no labels or ARIA attributes in this file.
notes: A self-contained ledger screen prototype for browsing workshop pieces in a phone-sized surface.
questions: Are the displayed counts meant to represent a larger dataset outside this file?
Should search, compose, or rail navigation have behavior here, or is this only a static screen?
Are the substrate descriptors such as stack, bands, field, and flow intended as formal fields?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file presents an All Pieces ledger screen with categories, sections, piece rows, counts, compose action, and navigation rail.
intention_signals: It seems intended to show a browsable mobile workshop ledger where pieces are grouped by time period, categorized by type, and summarized through compact design metadata.
novelty: The row subtitles combine layout terms, warmth values, takes, and screen counts as compact ledger descriptors.
contribution: Contributes a phone-scale workshop ledger pattern with category filters, sectioned piece lists, compact metadata subtitles, version tags, summary counts, and rail navigation styling.
summary_for_embedding: Mobile HTML prototype for an All Pieces workshop ledger with search text, category pills, generated piece rows, version labels, counts, compose action, and icon rail.
intention_for_embedding: Created to represent a compact browsing surface for workshop pieces and their design metadata within a phone UI shell.
